methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 15 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.7x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Herasight is a seed-stage biotech company that has raised $5M+. The valuation universe comprises 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector, including Xaira Therapeutics, Zeta Surgical, Abridge, Ultima Genomics, and Eikon Therapeutics.

Only one valuation method proved applicable: Stage-Progression. Using total capital raised of $5M as a proxy for seed-stage valuation, a standard 2.5x multiplier for progression to the next financing stage yields $12.5M. Peer-Median analysis was not feasible due to insufficient narrow-filter comparables—while 15 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 met same-stage or adjacent-stage criteria. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied because target revenue is not on file; the public median P/S of 6.7x across the three biotech tickers cannot be meaningfully deployed without this input.

The triangulated range is $11M to $14M, centered on $12M. This reflects reliance on a single method and thus carries material uncertainty. The analysis would benefit from either revenue disclosure (to unlock public-comp anchoring) or identification of additional narrow-stage private benchmarks. Key risks include execution risk inherent to seed-stage biotech and limited visibility into capital deployment and burn rates.
